inline_comments.message:
Move this check out of the try/catch block so it is not immediately caught and re-thrown.

Maybe the payload should be attached to the exception, or at least logged so that it can be debugged?
Long => long?  Also, the l suffix looks too much like 1; either leave it off (not necessary here) or use L
Personally I don't think business rules like this belong in POJOs (model layer) - they should be in the service/controller layer.
Exceeding the 120 column limit throughout this class
this looks like a possible windows cr/lf issue for the whole file?
This LoggingUtil class is similar to other LoggingUtil classes in AAI like in champ-service and gizmo - perhaps it should be moved to aai-common and used by all three

This class explicitly instantiates a publisher of type DMaaPEventPublisher.  This introduces a build-time dependency on DMaaP which would prevent the data-router from working with another back-end message bus technologies.

The goal of the event-client library is to remove this type of build-time dependency.  In fact, you shouldn't need this file at all.  If you instantiate the DMaaPEventPublisher via a beans.xml file, you will remove the build-time dependency and allow run-time configuration to specify which type of producer to use (DmaaP, Kafka, RabbitMQ, etc).

Have a look at how this is done for the EntityEventPolicy.  You can find an example of how a DMaapEventConsumer is instantiated via a beans file here:

https://gerrit.onap.org/r/gitweb?p=aai/test-config.git;a=blob;f=data-router/dynamic/conf/entity-event-policy.xml;h=ccd6648bf6acaf4ee7061e3a78726ae00d96698f;hb=HEAD
